x.name: Ray Dalio
x.bio: Official account of Ray Dalio, founder of Bridgewater Associates, author of #1 New York Times bestseller 'Principles,' professional mistake maker

tweets.text: My point is that you can significantly raise your probabilities of making the right decisions by open-mindedly triangulating with believable people. Even in a terrible situation, you can still raise your probabilities of making the right decisions by open-mindedly triangulating with believable people. #principleoftheday
You can watch the full episode here: https://t.co/i5SL8kFE35

There are five major forces that together, operate in a big cycle:

- Financial force – money, debts, markets, economy
- Domestic political force – internal order and disorder, wealth gaps, and conflict between the left and the right
- International world order force – great power conflicts as rising power challenges
- Humans’ inventiveness force– Advances in technology
- Acts of nature force – droughts, floods, and pandemics

We can use these forces to understand the reality that we are now living out, and to understand the risks of where we are headed. @theallinpod
https://t.co/tpFQfQCOSU

Take a minute and listen…California is functionally bankrupt. You can’t tax your way out of it. A California bankruptcy will wash over to all the states as they will be paying for the bailout. https://t.co/aSXF6nr9HK

Because of ineptitude, grift and back scratching. 

Hard problems like going to the moon can be a bit inefficient but the problem set attracts a level of competence that drowns out all other problems than the problem itself. https://t.co/RTHisEifEu

The pace of innovation is incredibly fast right now. 

It’s easier than ever to spot the companies standing still.
Academic rigor and merit! 🙌🏽 https://t.co/d80QGRpa4P

x.name: Cathie Wood
x.bio: Founder, CEO and CIO @ARKinvest. Thematic portfolio manager for disruptive innovation, mom, economist, and women's advocate. Disclosure: https://t.co/chxRD4oWOd

tweets.text:
At @ARKInvest, we’re always looking for new tools that can sharpen our research and improve how we make investment decisions. Prediction markets are not just a new derivatives market — they represent a powerful new way to quantify risk and surface forward-looking insights.

We’ve been working with the @Kalshi team to list markets on topics about which we are curious, from macroeconomic data to scientific milestones. We believe the wisdom of crowds will play an increasingly important role in 21st century investment decisions, and we are proud to partner with @MansourTarek_ and the team to scale this new financial infrastructure.
Awesome. https://t.co/lod21nVAmg

Everyone < 80 yrs old only knows crises responses of "Just print the USDs we need", but printing USDs into commodity shortages = kaboom https://t.co/IlRCFbwZmJ

...and yet Hormuz remains closed.

The US bragging about how many targets we've hit in Iran while Hormuz remains closed is the geopolitical equivalent of "Every kid gets a trophy", because Hormuz closure means the world economy is on the clock.

Another 2-3 weeks closed = trouble https://t.co/gnyQVfHfTx

Twitter likes to talk about TACO, and I do too.

But as @agnostoxxx so eloquently put it, this is now about NACHO.

Actually

Hormuz
Opening

Thinking out loud …

Trump was on Fox News a little while ago saying the Iranians asked for 7 days and he gave them 10 days.

7 days is good Friday. Was potentially attacking a Muslim nation on Christian holiday a consideration?

So he put it off until Monday, April 6th at 8 PM ET. That is the day after Easter. Coincidence?

Also, the time is curious as that is literally the start of the NCAA national championship game.

His top donor David Ellison owns Paramount (CBS) and they paid huge to broadcast this game. Does he want a “distraction” lower his ratings? Will this lead Trump to delay again?
